questions.question: What does `flex-grow` control?
options: How much an item grows when there's extra space
The initial size of an item
questions.explanation: `flex-grow` determines how much a flex item will grow relative to siblings when extra space exists.

questions.question: What is the default value of `flex-grow`?
questions.explanation: The default value is `0`, meaning items do NOT grow by default.

questions.question: If 3 items have flex-grow: 1 and the container has 300px extra space, how much does each grow?
questions.explanation: Total grow = 3. Each item's share = 1/3 × 300px = 100px.

questions.question: What's the shorthand pattern for equal-width columns?
questions.explanation: `flex: 1` sets flex-grow: 1 with flex-basis: 0, creating equal-width columns.

questions.question: Does flex-grow work when items are larger than the container?
options: No, there's no extra space to distribute
questions.explanation: `flex-grow` only distributes EXTRA space. If items overflow, there's no extra space to grow into.

questions.question: If one item has flex-grow: 2 and two others have flex-grow: 1, how is extra space divided?
options: The first item gets 2/4 (half), the others get 1/4 each
The first item gets 2/3, others get 1/3 each
The first item gets all extra space
questions.explanation: Total flex-grow = 2 + 1 + 1 = 4. The first item gets 2/4, each of the others gets 1/4 of the extra space.

questions.question: What happens when flex-grow is set on items with flex-wrap: wrap?
options: Items grow within their line, then wrap when no more space
questions.explanation: Each line in a wrapped flex container distributes its own extra space using flex-grow values.

questions.question: What is the relationship between flex-grow and flex-basis?
options: flex-basis sets the starting size; flex-grow adds to it
questions.explanation: flex-basis determines the initial size, and flex-grow distributes any extra space on top of that.

questions.question: If two items have flex-basis: 100px and flex-grow: 1, and the container is 400px wide, how wide is each item?
questions.explanation: Total basis = 200px. Extra space = 400px - 200px = 200px. Each item gets 100px extra. Total = 100px + 100px = 200px. Wait, let me recalculate: 200px extra ÷ 2 = 100px extra each. 100px basis + 100px extra = 200px each.

questions.question: What does flex-grow: 0 do?
options: Prevents the item from growing beyond its flex-basis
Removes the item from the flow
questions.explanation: flex-grow: 0 (the default) means the item will not grow, staying at its flex-basis size.

questions.question: Can flex-grow use negative values?
options: No, flex-grow must be a non-negative number
Yes, negative values make items shrink
questions.explanation: flex-grow accepts non-negative numbers only. Negative values are invalid.

questions.question: If all items have flex-grow: 1, how does the browser distribute extra space?
questions.explanation: When all items have the same flex-grow value, extra space is divided equally among them.

questions.question: What happens if flex-grow is set to a very high value like 999?
options: That item gets almost all the extra space
It behaves the same as flex-grow: 1
questions.explanation: flex-grow is proportional. A value of 999 compared to others with 1 means that item gets 999/1000 of the extra space.

questions.question: With flex-direction: column, what dimension does flex-grow affect?
options: Neither — flex-grow doesn't work with column
questions.explanation: flex-grow always affects the main axis. With flex-direction: column, the main axis is vertical.

questions.question: What is the minimum allowed value for flex-grow?
questions.explanation: The minimum allowed value for flex-grow is 0. Values can be any non-negative number (0, 0.5, 1, 2, etc.).

questions.question: If an item has flex-grow: 1 but no explicit flex-basis, what starting size does it use?
options: Its content size (flex-basis defaults to auto)
questions.explanation: With flex-basis: auto (default), the item starts at its content size, then grows from there.

questions.question: How does flex-grow interact with max-width?
options: max-width caps the growth — an item can't exceed it
max-width is ignored in flex containers
questions.explanation: An item will not grow past its max-width value, even if flex-grow would distribute more space to it.

questions.question: What does flex-grow: 0.5 mean?
options: The item grows at half the rate of an item with flex-grow: 1
questions.explanation: flex-grow accepts fractional values. An item with 0.5 will grow half as much as an item with 1.

questions.question: In the shorthand flex: 2 1 auto, what is the flex-grow value?
questions.explanation: In the flex shorthand (flex: grow shrink basis), the first value is flex-grow. So flex: 2 1 auto has flex-grow: 2.

questions.question: Why might you use flex-grow instead of width or flex-basis?
options: flex-grow dynamically distributes available space, adapting to different container sizes
questions.explanation: flex-grow is dynamic — it distributes whatever space is available, making layouts responsive without fixed sizes.
